title: core
ipyfernel
text:
from solveit_modal.modal import *


app = mk_app('solveit-sandbox')
img = mk_img()
sb = mk_sandbox(app, img, gpu='T4')
host,port = get_tunnel(sb)
inject_pubkey(sb, get_pubkey()); start_ssh(sb)
ssh = mk_ssh(host, port)
verify_ssh(ssh)

✔ started ssh service
系统：  Linux
主机名：modal
用户：  root
内核：  4.4.0
架构：  x86_64
系统类型：GNU/Linux




start_remote_kernel

def start_remote_kernel(
    ssh, # SSH caller from mk_ssh
    kernel_path:str='/root/.local/share/jupyter/runtime/kernel-ipyf.json', # kernel JSON path
)->None:

Start remote kernel and wait until ready.

start_remote_kernel(ssh)

✔ remote kernel ready: /root/.local/share/jupyter/runtime/kernel-ipyf.json





get_remote_python

def get_remote_python(
    ssh, # SSH caller from mk_ssh
)->str: # path to remote python

Return the path of Python on the remote machine.

p = get_remote_python(ssh); p

'/usr/local/bin/python3'



from ipyfernel.core import *


register_remote_kernel(remote_python=p)
connect_existing_kernel(f'{host}:{port}')

ipyf_remote_kernel is already a registered kernel
/app/data/.ssh/config file updated.
Successfully created connection file and forwarded ports!

Call self as a function.



mk_sandbox

def mk_sandbox(
    app, # modal.App to register the Sandbox with
    img, # modal.Image for the Sandbox environment
    timeout:int=600, # auto-terminate after this many seconds
    gpu:NoneType=None, # GPU type string (e.g. "T4", "A100") or None for no GPU
    secrets:NoneType=None, # dict of secrets (defaults to local solveit secrets)
):

Call self as a function.

sb = mk_sandbox(app, img, gpu='T4'); sb

Sandbox()





get_tunnel

def get_tunnel(
    sb
):

Call self as a function.

host,port = get_tunnel(sb); host,port

('r433.modal.host', 40039)





get_pubkey

def get_pubkey(
    
):

Call self as a function.



inject_pubkey

def inject_pubkey(
    sb, pubkey
):

Call self as a function.

inject_pubkey(sb, get_pubkey())




start_ssh

def start_ssh(
    sb
):

Call self as a function.

start_ssh(sb)

✔ started ssh service


Why this change: micromamba images keep Python outside the default PATH (~/.local/share/mamba/envs/...), so bare ssh commands can’t find python3, pip, etc. Rather than forcing every call through micromamba run or login shells, mk_ssh now:

Figures out where Python actually lives on the remote machine
Prepends that bin/ directory to PATH before every command

After this, ssh('python3 -c "print(1+1)"') and ssh('pip list') just work — no micromamba run prefix needed.
This is equivalent to what micromamba run does internally, but baked into the ssh helper so it’s transparent to callers.



mk_ssh

def mk_ssh(
    host, port
):

Call self as a function.

ssh = mk_ssh(host,port); ssh

<function __main__.mk_ssh.<locals>.<lambda>(*cmd)>





verify_ssh

def verify_ssh(
    ssh
):

Verify SSH connection to Sandbox and print system info.

verify_ssh(ssh)

内核：  4.4.0
架构：  x86_64
系统类型：GNU/Linux

title: solveit-modal
text: This file will become your README and also the index of your documentation.

title: solveit-modal
text:
Developer Guide
If you are new to using nbdev here are some useful pointers to get you started.

Install solveit_modal in Development mode
# make sure solveit_modal package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to solveit_modal
$ nbdev_prepare

title: solveit-modal
text:
Usage

Installation
Install latest from the GitHub repository:
$ pip install git+https://github.com/ForBo7/solveit-modal.git
or from conda
$ conda install -c ForBo7 solveit_modal
or from pypi
$ pip install solveit_modal


Documentation
Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub repository’s pages. Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on conda and pypi respectively.
